title: Protagon Identity Schema
description: A portable AI writing-voice identity file (.protagon.json) produced by the USAW.ai / Protagons platform. Contains 28 linguistic and psychological facets, personality axes, TTS configuration, and generation provenance.

$schema.description: URI pointing to this JSON Schema definition.

name.description: Display name of the voice or character.

slug.description: URL-safe slug derived from the name.

tagline.description: Short one-line tagline summarising the voice.

tags.description: Free-form tags for categorisation and search.

prose_description.description: Extended narrative description of the voice written in prose.

synthesized_prompt.description: Compiled system prompt that can be injected into an LLM to replicate this voice.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the prompt was compiled.

compiler.description: Identifier for the compiler that produced this prompt.

dimensions_hash.description: Hash of the dimension data used to generate the prompt, for cache-busting.

rhetorical_signature.description: Free-form object capturing the rhetorical signature of the voice (e.g. dominant strategies, stance markers).

signature_patterns.description: List of distinctive linguistic patterns that characterise this voice.

replication_recommendations.description: Human-readable guidance on how to replicate this voice in writing.

personality.description: Five bipolar personality axes, each a float from 0 to 1 where 0 is the left pole and 1 is the right pole.

tts_config.description: Text-to-speech configuration for voice synthesis providers.

voice_prompt.description: Natural-language description used by ElevenLabs voice design to synthesize a matching voice.

voice_id.description: Pre-assigned ElevenLabs voice ID, or null if not yet generated.

stability.description: Voice stability setting (0-1). Higher values produce more consistent output.

similarity_boost.description: Similarity boost setting (0-1). Higher values make the voice more closely match the target.

style.description: Style exaggeration (0-1). Higher values amplify the voice's stylistic characteristics.

use_speaker_boost.description: Whether to enable ElevenLabs speaker boost processing.

dimensions.description: The 28 analytical facets that define this voice. Each facet key maps to a free-form object containing the facet's extracted data.

rhetorical_architecture.description: Facet 1 — Rhetorical Architecture: argument structures, persuasive strategies, and rhetorical moves.

modal_epistemic_stance.description: Facet 2 — Modal & Epistemic Stance: certainty markers, hedging, and knowledge claims.

metadiscourse.description: Facet 3 — Metadiscourse: self-referential language and text-organising commentary.

audience_relationship.description: Facet 4 — Audience Relationship: reader address, assumed knowledge, and engagement stance.

genre_conventions.description: Facet 5 — Genre Conventions: adherence to or departure from genre norms.

cohesion_architecture.description: Facet 6 — Cohesion Architecture: connective tissue, transitions, and textual flow.

evaluative_language.description: Facet 7 — Evaluative Language: appraisal, judgment, and appreciation patterns.

identity_construction.description: Facet 8 — Identity Construction: self-presentation, ethos, and voice persona.

temporal_patterns.description: Facet 9 — Temporal Patterns: tense usage, time references, and narrative temporality.

visual_integration.description: Facet 10 — Visual Integration: imagery, formatting cues, and visual-textual interplay.

lexical_sophistication.description: Facet 11 — Lexical Sophistication: vocabulary range, register, and word-choice patterns.

engagement_devices.description: Facet 12 — Engagement Devices: hooks, questions, calls to action, and interactive elements.

intertextuality.description: Facet 13 — Intertextuality: references, allusions, and dialogic connections to other texts.

information_architecture.description: Facet 14 — Information Architecture: structural organisation, heading patterns, and information hierarchy.

prosodic_features.description: Facet 15 — Prosodic Features: rhythm, stress, intonation cues, and sentence cadence.

cultural_markers.description: Facet 16 — Cultural Markers: cultural references, idioms, and sociolinguistic signals.

emotional_architecture.description: Facet 17 — Emotional Architecture: affect patterns, emotional range, and sentiment flow.

formality_components.description: Facet 18 — Formality Components: register markers, politeness strategies, and code-switching.

psychological_trauma_markers.description: Facet 19 — Psychological Trauma Markers: trauma-informed linguistic patterns and coping language.

neurological_cognitive_patterns.description: Facet 20 — Neurological & Cognitive Patterns: cognitive style, processing indicators, and thought organisation.

mental_health_markers.description: Facet 21 — Mental Health Markers: well-being indicators, resilience language, and distress signals.

physical_health_state_markers.description: Facet 22 — Physical Health State Markers: somatic references, energy levels, and embodiment language.

age_developmental_markers.description: Facet 23 — Age & Developmental Markers: generational language, maturity indicators, and developmental stage cues.

personality_temperament_patterns.description: Facet 24 — Personality & Temperament Patterns: Big Five traits, temperament signals, and dispositional language.

facet_25_core_psychology.description: Facet 25 — Core Psychology: attachment style, defence mechanisms, and deep psychological patterns.

facet_26_motivational_architecture.description: Facet 26 — Motivational Architecture: drive patterns, goal orientation, and intrinsic/extrinsic motivation.

facet_27_worldview_deep_structure.description: Facet 27 — Worldview & Deep Structure: ontological assumptions, epistemological stance, and meaning-making frameworks.

facet_28_moral_ethical_framework.description: Facet 28 — Moral & Ethical Framework: moral foundations, ethical reasoning style, and value hierarchies.

writing_sample.description: Representative writing sample generated in or demonstrating this voice.

best_for.description: Recommended use cases for this voice.

summary.description: Short summary of what this voice is best suited for.

use_cases.description: List of specific use cases where this voice excels.

taxonomy.description: Classification within the voice library taxonomy.

parent_archetype.description: Parent archetype this voice is a variant of.

persona_axis.description: Position on a labelled persona spectrum.

label.description: Name of the persona axis (e.g. "Empathetic <-> Detached").

analysis.description: Metadata about the analysis that produced this voice profile.

schema_version_major.description: Major version of the analysis schema (e.g. 5 for V5).

total_fields.description: Total number of extracted data fields across all facets.

completeness_score.description: Fraction of expected fields that were successfully extracted (0-1).

confidence.description: Overall confidence score for the analysis (0-1).

sample_size.description: Number of writing samples used in the analysis.

total_words_analyzed.description: Total word count across all samples analysed.

deployment.description: Safety and deployment metadata for content moderation.

content_tier.description: Content safety tier: standard (general use), dark (fiction wrapper required), or adversarial (restricted).

requires_fiction_wrapper.description: Whether this voice must be used within an explicit fiction framing.

age_gate_required.description: Whether an age-gate should be shown before exposing this voice.

model_notes.description: Optional notes about model compatibility or special handling.

recommended_context.description: Recommended usage context: general (unrestricted) or fiction_only (creative fiction contexts only).

generation.description: Provenance information about how this file was generated.

generated_via.description: Identifier for the tool or pipeline that produced this file.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this file was generated.

provider.description: AI provider used for generation (e.g. "google"), or null if not API-generated.

model.description: AI model used for generation (e.g. "gemini-2.5-pro"), or null if not API-generated.

api_version.description: API version used for generation (e.g. "v1"), or null if not API-generated.

source.description: Canonical URL for this voice on the originating platform.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for this voice data.

author.description: Organisation or individual that created this voice.

integrations.description: Deployment state for third-party platform integrations. Populated by deploy endpoints or SDK.
integrations.$comment: Added in v1 (additive, backwards-compatible). See docs/deployment.md for field spec.

integration_record.description: Standard deployment record for a third-party platform integration.
